characters.name: Agents 1
characters.description: The Federal Agents 1Voiced byPeter FernandezSpeciesHumanGenderMaleFriendsThe GeneralEnemiesCourageMuriel BaggeLikesButtered ToastFirst appearanceInvisible Muriel Federal Agents 1are a squad of ninjas that patrol government-related operations. A common running gag when the agents appear would be the use of jungle music. It is possible these agents are employed by the US Government. After Courage finds a blue stone and gives it to Muriel that makes her invisible, the superior of the base asks the Federal Agents to find it and bring the person responsible. The Bag family is eating, until it looks like three ninjas in search of the stone, they take Muriel to interrogate her about the disappearance of the stone but she thinks that they want to know the dog food recipe that was competing.

characters.name: Ai Shen
characters.description: Evil EmpressAlso known asAuntie (byDi Lung)Voiced byWinnie Chaffee[1]SpeciesHumanGenderFemaleFamilyMei Ling(Twin Sister)Di Lung(Nephew)EnemiesCourageandMei LingLikesDeath, Murder, Torture and DestructionDislikesPeace and HarmonyFirst appearanceSquatting Tiger, Hidden DogAi Shen(also known asThe Evil Empress) is the evil ruler of China and the main antagonist in "Squatting Tiger, Hidden Dog". She speaks only in Chinese and has bound feet(which could be why she floats). She has weapons such as theHsu Quandaoand thenunchucks. In theepisode, she rules in her palace and her nephewDi Lungarrives. She tells Di Lung her source of power is draining away because the magic silk worm is dying. She commands him to find someone truly innocent and remove their bones to get her powers back. In the Great Wall of China,MurielandEustaceare talking to each other. Di Lung sneaks from behind and says, "Watch where you're going, ya fool!", pushingCourageoff the wall. Di Lung uses his pet frog to belch out a green bubble, trap Muriel and take her to the palace of theEvil Empress, as he himself follows. Courage uses Di Lung's frog and creates a bubble and heads to the palace as well. Muriel is seen chained and hanging from the ceiling (however, she thinks she's in a hammock), while Di Lung is ready to remove her bones (which Muriel thinks is a local tradition). Courage tells the Evil Empress that she doesn't want to remove Muriel's bones. Di Lung and the Empress banish Courage out of the palace (along with the box that had the magic silkworm, the source of the Empress' power).

characters.name: Alien Brain Boss
characters.description: Alien Brain BossVoiced byDaniel OreskesSpeciesBrain alienGenderMaleFriendsAlien Brain VisitorEnemiesMuriel BaggeColor(s)Blue brainPurple baseGreen tentaclesDislikesHumansEarthFirst appearanceCar Broke, Phone  Yes The Alien Brain Bossis an gigantic, blue brain that arrived in the town ofNowhereinside a UFO who's goals is the absorb juice of kindness to take over the universe. He appears as the main antagonist in the episodeCar Broke, Phone Yes, along with his henchman, theAlien Brain Visitor.

characters.name: Alien Brain Visitor
characters.description: Alien Brain VisitorVoiced byJon Adams[1]SpeciesBrain-alienGenderMaleFriendsAlien Brain BossEnemiesCourageMuriel BaggeAlliesAlien Brain BossColor(s)Blue Brain  with purple tentacles and yellow eyesFirst appearanceCar Broke, Phone YesQuote"Car broke. Phone, yes?"The Alien Brain Visitoris a small alien that works for theAlien Brain Boss. He appears in the episodeCar Broke, Phone Yes. The Alien Brain Visitor is simply a brain with three large purple tentacles that he uses for movement and holding things, and two large, bulging eyes attached to pink stalks. During the first few minutes of his appearance, he hides himself in a dark green trench coat far too big for him as well as a black hat with a brown band. The Alien Brain Visitor obedient and determined, diligently following the Alien Brain Boss' orders and using simple deception to get what he needs. Near the end of the episode, kindness in liquid form is thrown into his eyes and he becomes friendly and polite. He doesn't appear to know Earth's languages or technology very well, speaking in short, broken phrases usually ending in "yes," shaking his head in a "no" motion whenever he says "yes," and picking up a rotary phone by its base and speaking into it without dialing. The Alien Brain Visitor obedient and determined, diligently following the Alien Brain Boss' orders and using simple deception to get what he needs. Near the end of the episode, kindness in liquid form is thrown into his eyes and he becomes friendly and polite. He doesn't appear to know Earth's languages or technology very well, speaking in short, broken phrases usually ending in "yes," shaking his head in a "no" motion whenever he says "yes," and picking up a rotary phone by its base and speaking into it without dialing.

characters.name: Angry Mob
characters.description: Angry MobVoiced byVariousSpeciesHumanGenderMale (generally)FriendsEustace(initially)Courage(later)Bigfoot(later)Muriel(later)EnemiesEustace (later)Courage (initially)Bigfoot (initially)Muriel (initially)Color(s)Black bodiesYellow eyesFirst appearanceCourage Meets BigfootTheAngry Mobare a horde of people hired byEustaceto captureBigfootinCourage Meets Bigfoot. They appear connected, as they have glowing eyes not only on their heads, but on their torsos. The mob later finds out that Bigfoot has amommy. This gives the mob some sympathy towards Bigfoot. When Eustace catches Bigfoot and refuses to release him the mob then turn against him and chase him down instead.

characters.name: Asian Lung Dragon
characters.description: Asian Lung DragonSpeciesDragonGenderMaleFriendsEvil EmpressDi LungEnemiesCourageColor(s)GreenFirst appearanceSquatting Tiger, Hidden DogTheAsian Lung Dragonis a vicious Chinese dragon that guards theEvil Empress' palace inSquatting Tiger, Hidden Dog. In theepisode, Courage manages to get to the palace, but it is guarded by the Asian Lung Dragon and he breathes fire at Courage. TheGood Empressappears in her dragonfly form and she tells Courage to use his enemy's force against him. The dragon breathes fire again but Courage engulfs it in his mouth and he exhales out the flames back at the dragon, which is disintegrated into ashes. It is unknown if the dragon is truly dead or not, since he is a cartoon character.

characters.name: Aunt Gertrude
characters.description: Aunt GertrudeVoiced byMary TestaSpeciesHumanGenderFemaleFamilyMuriel Bagge(niece)Eustace Bagge(nephew-in-law)Dorothy(niece)Freaky Fred(grandnephew)Harold (son; possibly)Muriel's Parents (sibling and sibling-in-law)LikesVinegarAunt Gertrudeis the deceased aunt ofMuriel Bagge. Despite her being deceased, Gertrude was mentioned numerous times throughout the show. Her first appearance was inShirley the Medium, whereShirleyaccidentally called up her spirit, and it was shown that she and Muriel had shared an amiable familial relationship - they warmly inquired after each other's well-being, and Gertrude even advised Muriel to use vinegar in her culinary recipes. This eventually became one of the running gags throughout the series, where Muriel would always use too much vinegar, though strange to say, her cooking always turned out to be remarkably delicious because of it.

characters.name: Banana Leader
characters.description: Top BananaVoiced byPeter Francis JamesSpeciesMonkeyGenderMaleFamilyKing Kong(uncle)FriendsKing KongBanana People(formerly)EnemiesCourageBanana People (currently)First appearance1000 Years of Courage TheBanana Chief(also known as theTop Banana) is a banana costume worn by aMonkeywho was proclaimed as the leader of theBanana Republic. The costume is similar to the banana inhabitants, only that it has very large and white eyebrows. To demonstrate his title as the leader of the bananas he uses a red cape that is held with a grey medal with TB initials, "Top Banana".

characters.name: Banana Leader

characters.name: Banana People
characters.description: Banana PeopleSpeciesBananaGenderMaleFemaleFriendsCourageMuriel BaggeEustace BaggeEnemiesMonkeyKing KongCourage(formerly)Color(s)Yellow (newborn-adult)Brown (senior)First appearance1000 Years of Courage TheBanana Peopleappear in the episode1000 Years of Courage.Courageand the family encounter them after the Earth is hit by a meteor, spinning them 1000 years into the future, where this race of intelligent bananas has become the dominant form of life on Earth. These bananas inhabit theBanana Republic, which has apolice force, and are led by theTop Banana, who wears a cape. However, the leader is revealed to be amonkeyin disguise that acts on plot next to his uncleKing Kongto carry bananas to Bananahalla and then devour them. With some intervention of Courage, the bananas discover the truth and rebel against the primates.

characters.name: Banana Police
characters.description: Banana PoliceSpeciesMaleGenderBananaFriendsBanana PeopleBaby BananaMother BananaEnemiesCourage(formerly)Color(s)YellowBlueLikesLaw and orderDislikesCrimeFirst appearance1000 Years of Courage TheBanana Policeare Banana police officers who appeared in the episode1000 Years of Courage. They are the police force for theBanana Republicwho pursueCourageafter he is accused of trying to kidnap ababy bananafrom its mother. After Courage manages to avoid them, it appears they have handed theBanana People'wanted' posters of Courage as they are seen reading one later in the episode.

characters.name: Banana Suit Dealer
characters.description: Banana Suit DealerVoiced byYul VazquezSpeciesBananaEnemiesThe GeneralColor(s)YellowGreyLikesObjectsDealingFirst appearance1000 Years of CourageQuote“Come 'ere, I got somethin’ for ya.”TheBanana Suit Dealerfirst appeared in1000 Years of Courage. During the episode he sold banana suits toMuriel,EustaceandCourage. When they did not have any of the money used in the Banana Republic, he accepted bartering and made the exchange for a dead whale and an accordion from Courage, who holds off on giving him an anchor. The dealer accepts this before claiming Courage drives a hard bargain. Later, when Courage goes to the bathroom, the dealer sneaks up and steals Courage's anchor, then says Courage is a witness and must be disposed of before throwing Courage down a toilet. As there were wanted posters of Courage posted, presumably the banana dealer was also wanted for aiding and abetting a wanted man, which is why he suddenly turned on Courage.

characters.name: Barbara
characters.description: BarbaraSpeciesHumanGenderFemaleFriendsFred(ex-boyfriend)First appearanceFreaky Fred BarbarawasFred's girlfriend mentioned in the episodeFreaky Fred. While dating, Fred became obsessed with cutting hair and eventually shaved Barbara’s hair off, causing her to leave him. Her story is mentioned in a flashback in the episodeFreaky Fred.

characters.name: Basil
characters.description: BasilAlso known asCousin BasilVoiced byGeorge TaylorSpeciesHumanGenderMaleFamilyMuriel Bagge(supposedly)Eustace Bagge(supposedly)Courage(supposedly)Color(s)PurpleBlueBlackLikesCrumpetsFlounderHis familyDislikesThe Park RangersFirst appearanceThe Shadow of CourageQuotes“Zip your mouth shut, dog. Or I'll be fishing you again, I will!”SNARLING!!!!!Basil(profiled as criminal01144890CL) is a misunderstood burglar and main antagonist in the episodeFamily Business. Basil is slightly round with a blue long-sleeved shirt with dark purple stripes, black rubber gloves, black pants and shoes, purple beanie and black eye mask. He has a bit of stubble. Basil appears to suffer from some form of dementia that causes him to be disoriented in his surroundings, which leads him to rapidly switch personalities from a hard-boiled criminal to a doting family member. In either of his personas, Basil often does unorthodox actions. With his normal persona he is a career criminal, breaking into private property with intent to steal. His actions suggest prior experience and skill, as he severed telephone lines in the Bagge residence to stave off the forthcoming police alerts if discovered. Basil appears to suffer from some form of dementia that causes him to be disoriented in his surroundings, which leads him to rapidly switch personalities from a hard-boiled criminal to a doting family member. In either of his personas, Basil often does unorthodox actions. With his normal persona he is a career criminal, breaking into private property with intent to steal. His actions suggest prior experience and skill, as he severed telephone lines in the Bagge residence to stave off the forthcoming police alerts if discovered. However, Basil uses a fish to club his victims into submission, as opposed to most crooks who use a billy club, baseball bat or other blunt objects to intimidate civilians. He also uses rope to tie up his victims, or may scare one victim into tying up the others. When stealing from the Bagge residence, his memory lapsed and he thought he was with his own family, and started praising them and saying they should spend time together like they always should. A stressful situation, such as hearing a siren, caused his "usual" persona to resurface. When a situation has been relaxed for some time, his "family" persona resurfaces.
characters.personality_section: Basil appears to suffer from some form of dementia that causes him to be disoriented in his surroundings, which leads him to rapidly switch personalities from a hard-boiled criminal to a doting family member. In either of his personas, Basil often does unorthodox actions. With his normal persona he is a career criminal, breaking into private property with intent to steal. His actions suggest prior experience and skill, as he severed telephone lines in the Bagge residence to stave off the forthcoming police alerts if discovered. However, Basil uses a fish to club his victims into submission, as opposed to most crooks who use a billy club, baseball bat or other blunt objects to intimidate civilians. He also uses rope to tie up his victims, or may scare one victim into tying up the others. When stealing from the Bagge residence, his memory lapsed and he thought he was with his own family, and started praising them and saying they should spend time together like they always should. A stressful situation, such as hearing a siren, caused his "usual" persona to resurface. When a situation has been relaxed for some time, his "family" persona resurfaces.

characters.name: Basil
characters.description: BasilAlso known asCousin BasilVoiced byGeorge TaylorSpeciesHumanGenderMaleFamilyMuriel Bagge(supposedly)Eustace Bagge(supposedly)Courage(supposedly)Color(s)PurpleBlueBlackLikesCrumpetsFlounderHis familyDislikesThe Park RangersFirst appearanceThe Shadow of CourageQuotes“Zip your mouth shut, dog. Or I'll be fishing you again, I will!”SNARLING!!!!!Basil(profiled as criminal01144890CL) is a misunderstood burglar and main antagonist in the episodeFamily Business. Basil is slightly round with a blue long-sleeved shirt with dark purple stripes, black rubber gloves, black pants and shoes, purple beanie and black eye mask. He has a bit of stubble. Basil appears to suffer from some form of dementia that causes him to be disoriented in his surroundings, which leads him to rapidly switch personalities from a hard-boiled criminal to a doting family member. In either of his personas, Basil often does unorthodox actions. With his normal persona he is a career criminal, breaking into private property with intent to steal. His actions suggest prior experience and skill, as he severed telephone lines in the Bagge residence to stave off the forthcoming police alerts if discovered. characters.name: Bearded Man (character)
characters.description: Bearded ManVoiced byPeter Fernandez(Freaky Fred)John R. Dilworth(A Beaver's Tale)SpeciesHumanGenderMaleFamilyUnnamed FatherFriendsCourageFreaky FredEnemiesCourageColor(s)Orange BeardDislikesBarber ShopFirst appearanceFreaky FredQuotes“Oh, yeah. This here's the OI' Nowhere River. Yup, that's what it is, all right. Busted its banks for some reason. Must be something huge blocking it up. Somewhere up over there. Good thing I saved this. As long as I got this straw, I'm a motorboat.”“Just a trim partner.”Bearded Manis a minor character who first appears inFreaky Fred. When he visitsFreaky Fred’sbarber shop, Fred once again gives into his hair cutting compulsion and shaves off the man’s hair after the man asks only for a small trim.

characters.name: Benton Tarantella
characters.description: Benton TarantellaVoiced byPeter FernandezSpeciesZombie (formerly human)GenderMaleFriendsVan VolkheimMr. NastyCourageEustace(formerly, unless they're nasty enough to be his TV stars)EnemiesCourageEustaceMurielAlliesVan VolkheimFirst appearanceEveryone Wants to DirectQuote"Yes, yes."Benton Tarantellais an undead film director and the ex-partner ofErrol Von Volkheim. Benton is the main antagonist of the episodesEveryone Wants to DirectandAngry Nasty People. As a zombie, Tarantella holds the same desires he did as a serial killer when he was alive. His persuasive charm and reputable status enabled him to carry out his malicious plans without question, as he easily convinced Muriel and Eustace to participate in his movies. However, it appears Tarantella has a genuine passion for creating films with his attention to details in his scripts and extensive planning to film scenes, even though his ultimate goal is to satisfy his hunger for human flesh. Despite their arguments, Tarantella and his partner Van Volkheim seemed to be real friends. Tarantella resurrected Volkheim to share a meal and film with him again. When he reappeared later, this insidious desire for human flesh seemed to have gone away, and his malevolent disposition became centered around more directorial goals such as using other people's torture as a form of entertainment. characters.name: Big Bayou
characters.description: Big BayouVoiced byMichael C. GwynneSpeciesSnakeGenderMaleEnemiesCourageMurielSlugsColor(s)Red, Orange and GreenLikesHimselfFirst appearanceThe Uncommon ColdBig Bayouis the main antagonist of the episode "The Uncommon Cold". He is a red snake who is very vain and egotistical, as he forces his slaves to make "statues" of him from the skins he sheds. He was defeated by his own "statues" afterCourage(using Bayou magic) ordered them to attack him, scaring Big Bayou out of his wits. Far too self absorbed to attack images of himself he was forced to beg for mercy, but his pleas were ignored by the statues. He is later seen suffering and getting tortured, while locked with chains around him. The statues probably did this to him. He is a giant red venomous snake with strong fangs and a long snake tongue. He has yellow eyes typical of venomous snakes. His appearance and concept looks like mainly inspired by theBlack Mambabut he has the skin colour from red snakes like theCorn Snake. He is really selfish and narcissistic. He is an individualist and lives alone, caring only about himself. He loves his appearance and likes to keep sure of himself. He loves also his shed skins, in fact he does love them a lot. He also did this with his house, snake shaped and similar to him. He is evil towards everyone else, because he treats theSlugsas slaves in order to have a beautifullavorazione artigianaof his shed skins.

characters.name: Big Toe
characters.description: The Big ToeAlso known asThe Clutching FootFoot Fungus GangstersThe Giant FootVoiced byPaul Schoeffler(Big Toe)Arnold Stang(small toes)SpeciesFoot fungusGenderMaleFriendsKatzLe QuackCajun FoxBlack Puddle QueenWeremoleEustace Bagge(in "Ball of Revenge"; formerly)EnemiesCourageMuriel BaggeEustace BaggeColor(s)Purple with green festers and black nailsLikesCausing crimeFirst appearanceThe Clutching FootQuote"Or the fat lady gets it, see?"TheBig Toeis the main antagonist of the episode "The Clutching Foot". It is a strange foot fungus thatEustacehad gotten and somehow took over his body and formed into a stereotypical gangster/mafia-type character. It is a giant left foot with toes for heads and speaks like a stereotypical old-fashion mafia boss with big toe as kingpin and smaller toes as his minions. After taking over Eustace's body. They threaten to crushMurielwith the big toe ending with most sentences with "or the fat lady gets it", forcing Courage to commit many crimes such as bank robbery and crashing a train. Later, Courage defeats the Clutching Foot by licking it, thus freeing Eustace and Muriel. However, Courage had gotten similar talking fungus on his tongue.

characters.name: Bigfoot
characters.description: BigfootAlso known asTheodoreVoiced byJohn R. DilworthSpeciesBigfootGenderMaleFamilyOld Woman(mother)FriendsCourageMuriel BaggeEnemiesEustace BaggeColor(s)PinkPurpleRedBlueLikesBeing with his motherDancingDislikesBeing without his motherFirst appearanceCourage Meets BigfootQuote"Mommy!"Bigfoot(real name:Theodore) is a sasquatch that shows up at the farm inCourage Meets Bigfoot. Because of his appearance, Bigfoot is generally misconstrued as a savage beast. He is actually a very friendly and timid creature, that becomes a bit troublesome when he's frightened or lost. He lives with his mother, who can be a bit defensive of him. Bigfoot first made his way intoNowhereafter he decided to play outside of his backyard, against his mother's wishes. Late one night, he got into theBagges'garbage, and was seen byCourage. WhileMurielbelieved raccoons to be responsible for the mess, Courage had his doubts. Staying as look-out from the bedroom window, Courage heard Bigfoot roar during the night and rushed downstairs, only to find him trying to take Muriel's homemade pie from the windowsill. Because of his appearance, Bigfoot is generally misconstrued as a savage beast. He is actually a very friendly and timid creature, that becomes a bit troublesome when he's frightened or lost. He lives with his mother, who can be a bit defensive of him.
